quiz_questions.question_text: What is an element made up of?

options.text: Two or more different types of atoms bonded together
Only one type of atom that cannot be broken down further
A physical mixture of several different pure substances
Molecules that are loosely held together by weak forces

quiz_questions.explanation: An element is a pure substance made up of only one type of atom. It cannot be broken down into simpler substances by ordinary chemical means.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of a compound?

options.text: Pure gold
Oxygen gas
Table salt (NaCl)
A trail mix blend

quiz_questions.explanation: Table salt (NaCl) is a compound because it is made of two different elements — sodium and chlorine — chemically bonded together in a fixed ratio.

quiz_questions.question_text: How can the components of a mixture be separated?

options.text: Only by nuclear reactions in a laboratory setting
Only by chemical reactions that break down bonds
By physical methods such as filtering or sorting
Mixtures cannot be separated once they are combined

quiz_questions.explanation: Mixtures can be separated by physical methods such as filtration, evaporation, or magnetism because the components are not chemically bonded together.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a heterogeneous mixture?

options.text: Saltwater solution
Pure distilled water
A copper wire sample
A garden salad bowl

quiz_questions.explanation: A garden salad is a heterogeneous mixture because you can see and pick out the different components — lettuce, tomatoes, croutons — they are not evenly distributed.

quiz_questions.question_text: What type of substance is water (H2O)?

options.text: An element
A compound
A mixture
A solution

quiz_questions.explanation: Water is a compound because it is made of two different elements — hydrogen and oxygen — chemically bonded together in the fixed ratio H2O.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of an element?

options.text: Carbon dioxide
Baking soda
Aluminum foil
Lemonade drink

quiz_questions.explanation: Aluminum foil is made of the element aluminum (Al), which consists of only one type of atom. Carbon dioxide and baking soda are compounds; lemonade is a mixture.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the key difference between a compound and a mixture?

options.text: Compounds are always liquids while mixtures are always solids
Compounds have chemically bonded parts; mixtures do not
Mixtures contain only one element while compounds have many
Compounds can be separated physically but mixtures cannot

quiz_questions.explanation: In a compound, elements are chemically bonded in a fixed ratio and can only be separated by chemical means. In a mixture, substances are physically combined and can be separated by physical methods.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of these is a homogeneous mixture?

options.text: Oil and water in a glass
Sand mixed with pebbles
A bowl of vegetable soup
Saltwater in a container

quiz_questions.explanation: Saltwater is a homogeneous mixture (also called a solution) because the salt is evenly dissolved throughout the water, making it look uniform.

quiz_questions.question_text: How many elements are currently listed on the periodic table?

options.text: About 50 elements total
About 80 elements total
About 118 elements total
About 200 elements total

quiz_questions.explanation: The periodic table currently contains about 118 confirmed elements, ranging from hydrogen (the lightest) to oganesson (the heaviest).

quiz_questions.question_text: Can a compound be broken down into simpler substances?

options.text: Yes, but only by using chemical methods or reactions
No, compounds are the simplest form of all substances
Yes, simply by using physical separation like filtering
No, compounds and elements are exactly the same thing

quiz_questions.explanation: Compounds can be broken down into their component elements, but only through chemical reactions — not by physical methods like filtering or sorting.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is an element?

options.text: A pure substance made of only one type of atom
A mixture of two or more different types of atoms
A substance made by combining two other compounds
A physical blend of several substances mixed together

quiz_questions.explanation: An element is a pure substance consisting of only one type of atom. Elements cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a compound?

options.text: A substance made of only one kind of atom throughout
A substance made of two or more elements chemically bonded
A physical blend of substances that can be easily sorted
Any solid material found naturally occurring on the Earth

quiz_questions.explanation: A compound is a pure substance made of two or more different elements that are chemically bonded together in a fixed ratio, such as water (H2O) or carbon dioxide (CO2).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a mixture?

options.text: A single element found on the periodic table of elements
A substance that can never be separated into other parts
A combination of substances not chemically bonded together
Two elements that are chemically bonded in a fixed ratio

quiz_questions.explanation: A mixture is a combination of two or more substances that are physically combined but not chemically bonded. Each substance retains its own properties.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a homogeneous mixture?

options.text: A mixture where different parts can be seen clearly
A mixture where substances are bonded together tightly
A mixture that cannot ever be separated by any method
A mixture that appears uniform and the same throughout

quiz_questions.explanation: A homogeneous mixture has a uniform composition throughout — you cannot distinguish the individual components. Saltwater and air are common examples.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a heterogeneous mixture?

options.text: A mixture where you can see or identify different parts
A mixture that looks completely the same in all areas
A compound made of two chemically bonded substances
An element that exists in more than one physical form

quiz_questions.explanation: A heterogeneous mixture is not uniform — you can see or identify the different components. Trail mix and pizza are everyday examples.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a pure substance?

options.text: Any material found in nature without human processing
A material with a definite and constant composition throughout
A substance that can only exist as a solid at room temperature
A substance that is completely clear and transparent to light

quiz_questions.explanation: A pure substance has a definite, constant composition. Elements and compounds are both pure substances — elements have one type of atom, compounds have atoms bonded in a fixed ratio.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'chemical bond' mean?

options.text: The physical space that exists between two nearby atoms
The color change that happens when substances are combined
A force of attraction that holds atoms together in a substance
The measurement of how heavy a particular molecule becomes

quiz_questions.explanation: A chemical bond is an attractive force that holds atoms together within a compound. Breaking or forming chemical bonds requires or releases energy.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a solution?

options.text: A heterogeneous mixture with visible separate layers
A compound that can be separated by physical means
A homogeneous mixture where one substance dissolves in another
An element that exists in both solid and liquid forms at once

quiz_questions.explanation: A solution is a type of homogeneous mixture in which one substance (the solute) is evenly dissolved in another substance (the solvent).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'chemical formula' mean?

options.text: A shorthand notation showing the elements and their ratios in a compound
A detailed drawing of how atoms are arranged in three-dimensional space
A written description of how to perform a specific laboratory experiment
A list of all the physical properties that belong to a given pure element

quiz_questions.explanation: A chemical formula uses element symbols and subscript numbers to show which elements are in a compound and how many atoms of each are present — for example, H2O or CO2.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'periodic table' mean?

options.text: A chart that organizes all known elements by their properties
A list of every chemical reaction that has ever been recorded
A schedule showing when different chemicals should be combined
A table that records the results from a chemistry experiment

quiz_questions.explanation: The periodic table is a chart that organizes all known elements by increasing atomic number and groups them by similar chemical and physical properties.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student has a clear liquid that could be pure water or saltwater. Which test would best determine if it is a mixture or pure substance?

options.text: Look at the color of the liquid under bright light
Evaporate the liquid and check for any solid residue
Shake the container vigorously for about one minute
Measure the total volume of liquid in the container

quiz_questions.explanation: By evaporating the liquid, you can check if any solid residue (like salt crystals) remains. Pure water would leave nothing behind, while saltwater would leave salt crystals.

quiz_questions.question_text: A jeweler is told a ring is pure gold. She tests it and finds it contains gold and copper atoms mixed together. What type of substance is the ring?

options.text: The ring is a pure element made of gold atoms
The ring is a homogeneous mixture known as an alloy
The ring is a heterogeneous mixture of two metals
The ring is a compound with a fixed chemical formula

quiz_questions.explanation: A gold-copper ring is an alloy, which is a homogeneous mixture of metals. The metals are physically blended, not chemically bonded in a fixed ratio like a compound.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student pours sand into water and stirs. The sand settles to the bottom. What type of mixture is this?

options.text: It is a compound because two substances were combined together
It is a homogeneous mixture since both parts are still present
It is a heterogeneous mixture because you can see both parts
It is a pure substance because the sand did not dissolve fully

quiz_questions.explanation: Sand and water form a heterogeneous mixture because the two components are visibly distinct — the sand settles and does not dissolve uniformly in the water.

quiz_questions.question_text: A scientist uses electricity to split water into hydrogen gas and oxygen gas. What does this experiment demonstrate?

options.text: Water is a mixture that can be separated by filtration methods
Water is an element that contains only one type of atom inside
Water is a pure substance that cannot be broken down at all
Water is a compound that can be decomposed into its elements

quiz_questions.explanation: Electrolysis breaks the chemical bonds in water, separating it into hydrogen and oxygen gases. This proves water is a compound, not an element, because it can be decomposed into simpler substances.

quiz_questions.question_text: You stir sugar into hot tea until it completely dissolves and looks clear. What type of mixture have you created?

options.text: A heterogeneous mixture because two things were combined
A homogeneous mixture because it looks uniform throughout
A new compound because the sugar changed into something else
A pure element because the sugar is no longer visible now

quiz_questions.explanation: When sugar dissolves completely in tea, it forms a homogeneous mixture (solution) because the composition is uniform throughout — you cannot see the individual sugar particles.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student tries to separate salt from water by using a coffee filter. The salt passes through with the water. Why didn't this method work?

options.text: Salt is an element and elements cannot be filtered out of water
Salt is dissolved in the water and the particles are too small to filter
The coffee filter was too thick and should have been made thinner
Salt and water formed a compound that cannot be taken apart at all

quiz_questions.explanation: Dissolved salt particles are too small to be caught by a filter. To separate salt from water, evaporation would be a better method — the water evaporates and the salt crystals remain.

quiz_questions.question_text: You find a shiny rock that contains visible gold specks embedded in white quartz. How would you classify this rock?

options.text: It is a compound because gold and quartz are bonded together
It is a pure element because it contains gold atoms inside it
It is a heterogeneous mixture because you can see distinct parts
It is a homogeneous mixture because everything is in one piece

quiz_questions.explanation: The rock is a heterogeneous mixture because you can visually distinguish the gold specks from the white quartz. The components are not uniformly distributed.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student says that air is an element because it is invisible. What is wrong with this reasoning?

options.text: Air is actually a compound made of nitrogen and oxygen bonded together
Air is a homogeneous mixture of several gases including nitrogen and oxygen
Air is a heterogeneous mixture where you can see each different gas layer
Air is a pure substance because it always has the exact same composition

quiz_questions.explanation: Air is a homogeneous mixture of gases, primarily nitrogen and oxygen, along with small amounts of argon, carbon dioxide, and other gases. Being invisible does not make something an element.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a chemistry lab, a student heats sugar strongly and it turns black and releases steam. What does this suggest about sugar?

options.text: Sugar is a mixture that separated into its different components
Sugar is an element that changed color when heated to high levels
Sugar is a compound that decomposed into simpler substances with heat
Sugar is a pure substance that simply melted into a different state form

quiz_questions.explanation: When sugar is heated strongly, it undergoes a chemical reaction called decomposition, breaking down into carbon (the black residue) and water (the steam). This shows sugar is a compound.

quiz_questions.question_text: A recipe calls for mixing flour, sugar, eggs, and butter. A student says this creates a compound. Why is the student incorrect?

options.text: The ingredients are physically combined, not chemically bonded together
The ingredients are all elements that cannot form any chemical bonds
The ingredients form a solution that is uniform in all of its parts
The mixture has too many ingredients to qualify as a real compound

quiz_questions.explanation: Mixing batter ingredients creates a mixture, not a compound. The ingredients are physically combined and could theoretically be separated — they are not chemically bonded in a fixed ratio.
